name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЫҘ Щ„ЩҺШӘЩҺЩҶШІЩҗЩҠЩ„ЩҸ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘Щҗ ЩұЩ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺЩҖЩ°Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ©Щў)
```

```english
This is indeed [a Book] sent down by the Lord of all the worlds, (192)
```

```arabic
ЩҶЩҺШІЩҺЩ„ЩҺ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ ЩұЩ„ШұЩ‘ЩҸЩҲШӯЩҸ ЩұЩ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҸ (ЩЎЩ©ЩЈ)
```

```english
brought down by the Trustworthy Spirit (193)
```

```arabic
Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩүЩ° ЩӮЩҺЩ„Щ’ШЁЩҗЩғЩҺ Щ„ЩҗШӘЩҺЩғЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸЩҶШ°ЩҗШұЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ©ЩӨ)
```

```english
upon your heart (so that you may be one of the warners), (194)
```

```arabic
ШЁЩҗЩ„ЩҗШіЩҺШ§ЩҶЩҚ Ш№ЩҺШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩүЩ‘ЩҚ Щ…Щ‘ЩҸШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ (ЩЎЩ©ЩҘ)
```

```english
in a clear Arabic language. (195)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЫҘ Щ„ЩҺЩҒЩҗЩү ШІЩҸШЁЩҸШұЩҗ ЩұЩ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩҲЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ©ЩҰ)
```

```english
It is indeed [foretold] in the scriptures of the ancients. (196)
```

```arabic
ШЈЩҺЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…Щ’ ЩҠЩҺЩғЩҸЩҶ Щ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШЎЩҺШ§ЩҠЩҺШ©ЩӢ ШЈЩҺЩҶ ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҮЩҸЫҘ Ш№ЩҸЩ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҖЩ°Щ“ШӨЩҸШ§Ыҹ ШЁЩҺЩҶЩҗЩүЩ“ ШҘЩҗШіЩ’ШұЩҺЩ°Щ“ШЎЩҗЩҠЩ„ЩҺ (ЩЎЩ©Щ§)
```

```english
Is it not a sign for them that the learned of the Children of Israel recognize it? (197)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ ЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶЩҺЩҖЩ°ЩҮЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩүЩ° ШЁЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¶Щҗ ЩұЩ„Щ’ШЈЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ©ЩЁ)
```

```english
Had We sent it down upon some non-Arab (198)
```

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺЩӮЩҺШұЩҺШЈЩҺЩҮЩҸЫҘ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗЩ… Щ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩғЩҺШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗЫҰ Щ…ЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ©Щ©)
```

```english
and had he recited it to them, they would not have believed in it. (199)
```

```arabic
ЩғЩҺШ°ЩҺЩ°Щ„ЩҗЩғЩҺ ШіЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩғЩ’ЩҶЩҺЩҖЩ°ЩҮЩҸ ЩҒЩҗЩү ЩӮЩҸЩ„ЩҸЩҲШЁЩҗ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШ¬Щ’ШұЩҗЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩ Щ )
```

```english
This is how We let it pass through the hearts of the guilty: (200)
```

```arabic
Щ„ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗЫҰ ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩүЩ° ЩҠЩҺШұЩҺЩҲЩҸШ§Ыҹ ЩұЩ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺШ°ЩҺШ§ШЁЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҺ (ЩўЩ ЩЎ)
```

```english
they do not believe in it until they sight the painful punishment. (201)
```

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺЩҠЩҺШЈЩ’ШӘЩҗЩҠЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ… ШЁЩҺШәЩ’ШӘЩҺШ©ЩӢ ЩҲЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺШҙЩ’Ш№ЩҸШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩ Щў)
```

```english
It will overtake them suddenly while they are unaware. (202)
```

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ ЩҮЩҺЩ„Щ’ ЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Щ…ЩҸЩҶШёЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩ ЩЈ)
```

```english
Thereupon they will say, вҖҳShall we be granted any respite?вҖҷ (203)
```

```arabic
ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺШЁЩҗШ№ЩҺШ°ЩҺШ§ШЁЩҗЩҶЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩ ЩӨ)
```

```english
So do they seek to hasten on Our punishment? (204)
```

```arabic
ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҺШЎЩҺЩҠЩ’ШӘЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩҶ Щ…Щ‘ЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШ№Щ’ЩҶЩҺЩҖЩ°ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШіЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩ ЩҘ)
```

```english
Tell me, should We let them enjoy for some years, (205)
```

```arabic
Ш«ЩҸЩ…Щ‘ЩҺ Ш¬ЩҺШ§Щ“ШЎЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ… Щ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩғЩҺШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ ЩҠЩҸЩҲШ№ЩҺШҜЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩ ЩҰ)
```

```english
then there comes to them what they have been promised, (206)
```

```arabic
Щ…ЩҺШ§Щ“ ШЈЩҺШәЩ’ЩҶЩҺЩүЩ° Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ… Щ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩғЩҺШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ ЩҠЩҸЩ…ЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШ№ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩ Щ§)
```

```english
of what avail to them will be that which they were given to enjoy? (207)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ§Щ“ ШЈЩҺЩҮЩ’Щ„ЩҺЩғЩ’ЩҶЩҺШ§ Щ…ЩҗЩҶ ЩӮЩҺШұЩ’ЩҠЩҺШ©ЩҚ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҺШ§ Щ…ЩҸЩҶШ°ЩҗШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩ ЩЁ)
```

```english
We have not destroyed any town without its having warners, (208)
```

```arabic
Ш°ЩҗЩғЩ’ШұЩҺЩүЩ° ЩҲЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ§ ЩғЩҸЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ ШёЩҺЩҖЩ°Щ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩ Щ©)
```

```english
for the sake of admonition, and We were not unjust. (209)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ§ ШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҺШӘЩ’ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ ЩұЩ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҺЩҠЩҺЩҖЩ°Ш·ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҸ (ЩўЩЎЩ )
```

```english
It has not been brought down by the devils. (210)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺЩҶЫўШЁЩҺШәЩҗЩү Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҲЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’ШӘЩҺШ·ЩҗЩҠШ№ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩЎЩЎ)
```

```english
Neither does it behoove them, nor are they capable [of doing that]. (211)
```

```arabic
ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩҗ ЩұЩ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Щ’Ш№Щҗ Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ№Щ’ШІЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩЎЩў)
```

```english
Indeed, they are kept at bay [even] from hearing it. (212)
```

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺЩ„ЩҺШ§ ШӘЩҺШҜЩ’Ш№ЩҸ Щ…ЩҺШ№ЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҗ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҺЩҖЩ°ЩҮЩӢШ§ ШЎЩҺШ§Ш®ЩҺШұЩҺ ЩҒЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШ№ЩҺШ°Щ‘ЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩЎЩЈ)
```

```english
So do not invoke any god besides Allah, lest you should be among the punished. (213)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶШ°ЩҗШұЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШҙЩҗЩҠШұЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩӮЩ’ШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩЎЩӨ)
```

```english
Warn the nearest of your kinsfolk, (214)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺЩұШ®Щ’ЩҒЩҗШ¶Щ’ Ш¬ЩҺЩҶЩҺШ§ШӯЩҺЩғЩҺ Щ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩҗ ЩұШӘЩ‘ЩҺШЁЩҺШ№ЩҺЩғЩҺ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩЎЩҘ)
```

```english
and lower your wing to the faithful who follow you. (215)
```

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШөЩҺЩҲЩ’ЩғЩҺ ЩҒЩҺЩӮЩҸЩ„Щ’ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҗЩү ШЁЩҺШұЩҗЩүЩ“ШЎЩҢ Щ…Щ‘ЩҗЩ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩЎЩҰ)
```

```english
But if they disobey you, say, вҖҳI am absolved of what you do.вҖҷ (216)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШӘЩҺЩҲЩҺЩғЩ‘ЩҺЩ„Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩү ЩұЩ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺШІЩҗЩҠШІЩҗ ЩұЩ„ШұЩ‘ЩҺШӯЩҗЩҠЩ…Щҗ (ЩўЩЎЩ§)
```

```english
And put your trust in the All-mighty, the All-merciful, (217)
```

```arabic
ЩұЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩү ЩҠЩҺШұЩҺЩүЩ°ЩғЩҺ ШӯЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШӘЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ…ЩҸ (ЩўЩЎЩЁ)
```

```english
who sees you when you stand [for prayer], (218)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШӘЩҺЩӮЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҸШЁЩҺЩғЩҺ ЩҒЩҗЩү ЩұЩ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺЩҖЩ°Ш¬ЩҗШҜЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩЎЩ©)
```

```english
and your going about among those who prostrate. (219)
```

```arabic
ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЫҘ ЩҮЩҸЩҲЩҺ ЩұЩ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠШ№ЩҸ ЩұЩ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҸ (ЩўЩўЩ )
```

```english
Indeed He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (220)
```

```arabic
ЩҮЩҺЩ„Щ’ ШЈЩҸЩҶЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗШҰЩҸЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩүЩ° Щ…ЩҺЩҶ ШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҸ ЩұЩ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҺЩҠЩҺЩҖЩ°Ш·ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҸ (ЩўЩўЩЎ)
```

```english
Should I inform you on whom the devils descend? (221)
```

```arabic
ШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩүЩ° ЩғЩҸЩ„Щ‘Щҗ ШЈЩҺЩҒЩ‘ЩҺШ§ЩғЩҚ ШЈЩҺШ«ЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҚ (ЩўЩўЩў)
```

```english
They descend on every sinful liar. (222)
```

```arabic
ЩҠЩҸЩ„Щ’ЩӮЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩұЩ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Щ’Ш№ЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩғЩ’Ш«ЩҺШұЩҸЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩғЩҺЩҖЩ°Ш°ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩўЩЈ)
```

```english
They eavesdrop, and most of them are liars. (223)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺЩұЩ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҸШ№ЩҺШұЩҺШ§Щ“ШЎЩҸ ЩҠЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШЁЩҗШ№ЩҸЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ ЩұЩ„Щ’ШәЩҺШ§ЩҲЩҸЫҘЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩўЩӨ)
```

```english
As for the poets, [only] the perverse follow them. (224)
```

```arabic
ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…Щ’ ШӘЩҺШұЩҺ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҒЩҗЩү ЩғЩҸЩ„Щ‘Щҗ ЩҲЩҺШ§ШҜЩҚ ЩҠЩҺЩҮЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩўЩҘ)
```

```english
Have you not regarded that they rove in every valley, (225)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ…ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺЩҒЩ’Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩўЩҰ)
```

```english
and that they say what they do not do? (226)
```

```arabic
ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩұЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШЎЩҺШ§Щ…ЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ ЩҲЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ ЩұЩ„ШөЩ‘ЩҺЩҖЩ°Щ„ЩҗШӯЩҺЩҖЩ°ШӘЩҗ ЩҲЩҺШ°ЩҺЩғЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ ЩұЩ„Щ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҺ ЩғЩҺШ«ЩҗЩҠШұЩӢШ§ ЩҲЩҺЩұЩҶШӘЩҺШөЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЫў ШЁЩҺШ№Щ’ШҜЩҗ Щ…ЩҺШ§ ШёЩҸЩ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ Ы— ЩҲЩҺШіЩҺЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸ ЩұЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШёЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸЩҲЩ“Ш§Ыҹ ШЈЩҺЩүЩ‘ЩҺ Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩӮЩҺЩ„ЩҺШЁЩҚ ЩҠЩҺЩҶЩӮЩҺЩ„ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩўЩўЩ§)
```

```english
Barring those who have faith, do righteous deeds, and remember Allah much often, and vindicate themselves after they have been wronged. And the wrongdoers will soon know at what goal they will end up. (227)
```

## (Commentary)

The verses point toward what serves as a conclusion derived from the seven preceding stories, encompassing a rebuke and a threat directed at the disbelievers (ЩғЩҒШ§Шұ) of the nation.

Within them is a defense of the prophethood (ЩҶШЁЩҲШ©) of the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ by arguing for it through its mention in the scriptures of the ancients (ШІШЁШұ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҲЩ„ЩҠЩҶ) and the knowledge of the scholars of the Children of Israel (Ш№Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎ ШЁЩҶЩҠ ШҘШіШұШ§ШҰЩҠЩ„) regarding him. There is also a defense of his Book by arguing that it is neither from the promptings of the devils (Ш§Щ„ШҙЩҠШ§Ш·ЩҠЩҶ) nor from the utterances of poets (Ш§Щ„ШҙШ№ШұШ§ШЎ).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸ Щ„ЩҺШӘЩҺЩҶЩ’ШІЩҗЩҠЩ„ЩҸ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№Ш§Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *And indeed, it is a revelation from the Lord of all the worlds (192).* The pronoun refers to the Quran. In this, there is a return to what was mentioned at the beginning of the Surah in His saying: **ШӘЩҗЩ„Щ’ЩғЩҺ ШўЩҠШ§ШӘЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩғЩҗШӘШ§ШЁЩҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҗ** вҖ” *These are the signs of the Manifest Book (2).* It also serves as a follow-up to the discussion of their disbelief in it, as in His subsequent saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ЩҠЩҺШЈЩ’ШӘЩҗЩҠЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ Ш°ЩҗЩғЩ’ШұЩҚ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„ШұЩ‘ЩҺШӯЩ’Щ…ЩҶЩҗ Щ…ЩҸШӯЩ’ШҜЩҺШ«ЩҚ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҮЩҸ Щ…ЩҸШ№Щ’ШұЩҗШ¶ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШҢ ЩҒЩҺЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ ЩғЩҺШ°Щ‘ЩҺШЁЩҸЩҲШ§** вҖ” *There does not come to them any new reminder from the All-Beneficent but that they disregard it. They have already denied [it]... (5-6).*

The terms "sending down" (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҶШІЩҠЩ„) and "descending" (Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҶШІШ§Щ„) carry the same meaning, except that the form *ifвҖҳДҒl* (ШҘЩҒШ№Ш§Щ„) usually denotes a sudden, all-at-once occurrence, while the form *tafвҖҳД«l* (ШӘЩҒШ№ЩҠЩ„) denotes a gradual process. The linguistic origin of "descent" (Ш§Щ„ЩҶШІЩҲЩ„) regarding physical bodies is the movement of a body from a high place to a lower one; regarding non-physical entities, it is used in a manner appropriate to them.

His "sending down" (ШӘЩҶШІЩҠЩ„ЩҮ), the Exalted, is His bringing a thing forth from His presence to the realm of creation (Ш§Щ„Ш®Щ„ЩӮ) and determination (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩӮШҜЩҠШұ). He has described Himself as the All-Exalted (Ш§Щ„Ш№Щ„ЩҠ), the All-Magnificent (Ш§Щ„Ш№ШёЩҠЩ…), the All-Great (Ш§Щ„ЩғШЁЩҠШұ), the Most High (Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„), the Exalter of Ranks (ШұЩҒЩҠШ№ Ш§Щ„ШҜШұШ¬Ш§ШӘ), and the Subduer (Ш§Щ„ЩӮШ§ЩҮШұ) above His servants. Thus, the emergence of a thing by His bringing it into existence from His presence into the world of creation and determinationвҖ”or if you prefer, His bringing it forth from the world of the Unseen (Ш№Ш§Щ„Щ… Ш§Щ„ШәЩҠШЁ) to the world of the Seen (Ш№Ш§Щ„Щ… Ш§Щ„ШҙЩҮШ§ШҜШ©)вҖ”is considered a "sending down" (ШӘЩҶШІЩҠЩ„) from Him, the Exalted.

"Descending" (Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҶШІШ§Щ„) and "sending down" (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҶШІЩҠЩ„) have been used in His speech, the Exalted, regarding various things with this specific nuance, such as His saying: **ЩҠШ§ ШЁЩҺЩҶЩҗЩҠ ШўШҜЩҺЩ…ЩҺ ЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШІЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶШ§ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҗШЁШ§ШіШ§ЩӢ ЩҠЩҸЩҲШ§ШұЩҗЩҠ ШіЩҺЩҲЩ’ШўШӘЩҗЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’** вҖ” *O Children of Adam! We have certainly sent down to you garments to cover your nakedness (al-A'raf: 26)*; and His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШІЩҺЩ„ЩҺ Щ„ЩҺЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’Ш№Ш§Щ…Щҗ Ш«ЩҺЩ…Ш§ЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҺШ©ЩҺ ШЈЩҺШІЩ’ЩҲШ§Ш¬ЩҚ** вҖ” *...and He sent down for you eight mates of the cattle (az-Zumar: 6)*; and His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШІЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶЩҺШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШӯЩҺШҜЩҗЩҠШҜЩҺ ЩҒЩҗЩҠЩҮЩҗ ШЁЩҺШЈЩ’ШіЩҢ ШҙЩҺШҜЩҗЩҠШҜЩҢ** вҖ” *...and We sent down iron, wherein is great might (al-Hadid: 25)*; and His saying: **Щ…Ш§ ЩҠЩҺЩҲЩҺШҜЩ‘ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ЩғЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШЈЩҺЩҮЩ’Щ„Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩғЩҗШӘШ§ШЁЩҗ ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШҙЩ’ШұЩҗЩғЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩҠЩҸЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҺ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ Ш®ЩҺЩҠЩ’ШұЩҚ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’** вҖ” *Neither the faithless from among the People of the Book nor the polytheists wish that any good should be sent down to you from your Lord (al-Baqarah: 105).* He stated this generally in His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШҙЩҺЩҠЩ’ШЎЩҚ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Ш№ЩҗЩҶЩ’ШҜЩҺЩҶШ§ Ш®ЩҺШІШ§ШҰЩҗЩҶЩҸЩҮЩҸ ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ЩҶЩҸЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҮЩҸ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ШЁЩҗЩӮЩҺШҜЩҺШұЩҚ Щ…ЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҸЩҲЩ…ЩҚ** вҖ” *There is not a thing but its treasures are with Us, and We do not send it down except in a known measure (al-Hijr: 21).*

Among the verses indicating this meaning specifically regarding the Quran is His saying: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Ш¬ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ ЩӮЩҸШұЩ’ШўЩҶШ§ЩӢ Ш№ЩҺШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩӢШ§ Щ„ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҺЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’ЩӮЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ ШЈЩҸЩ…Щ‘Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩғЩҗШӘШ§ШЁЩҗ Щ„ЩҺШҜЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҶШ§ Щ„ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩҢ ШӯЩҺЩғЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҢ** вҖ” *We have made it an Arabic Quran so that you may exercise your reason. And indeed it is in the Mother of the Book with Us, surely exalted and wise (az-Zukhruf: 3-4).*

The "sending down" (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҶШІЩҠЩ„) was attributed to the "Lord of all the worlds" (ШұШЁ Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҠЩҶ) to indicate the monotheism (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҲШӯЩҠШҜ) вҖ” *the absolute oneness of Allah in His essence, attributes, and acts* вҖ” of the Lord, the Exalted, because it has been repeatedly mentioned that the polytheists (Ш§Щ„Щ…ШҙШұЩғЩҲЩҶ) only acknowledged Him as the Lord of lords, but did not view Him as the Lord of all the worlds.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҶЩҺШІЩҺЩ„ЩҺ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШұЩ‘ЩҸЩҲШӯЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү ЩӮЩҺЩ„Щ’ШЁЩҗЩғЩҺ Щ„ЩҗШӘЩҺЩғЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШЁЩҗЩ„ЩҗШіШ§ЩҶЩҚ Ш№ЩҺШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩҚ Щ…ЩҸШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ** вҖ” *The Trustworthy Spirit has brought it down to your heart, so that you may be one of the warners, in a clear Arabic language (193-195).* What is meant by the Trustworthy Spirit (Ш§Щ„ШұЩҲШӯ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩ…ЩҠЩҶ) is Gabriel (Ш¬ШЁШұШҰЩҠЩ„), the angel of revelation (Щ…Щ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШӯЩҠ), based on the evidence of His saying: **Щ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҺ Ш№ЩҺШҜЩҸЩҲЩ‘ЩӢШ§ Щ„ЩҗШ¬ЩҗШЁЩ’ШұЩҗЩҠЩ„ЩҺ ЩҒЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸ ЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү ЩӮЩҺЩ„Щ’ШЁЩҗЩғЩҺ ШЁЩҗШҘЩҗШ°Щ’ЩҶЩҗ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ** вҖ” *[Say,] вҖҳWhoever is an enemy of Gabriel [should know that] it is he who has brought it down to your heart by AllahвҖҷs willвҖҷ (al-Baqarah: 97).* He named him in another place the Holy Spirit (ШұЩҲШӯ Ш§Щ„ЩӮШҜШі): **ЩӮЩҸЩ„ ЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸ ШұЩҸЩҲШӯЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩӮЩҸШҜЩҸШіЩҗ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩғЩҺ ШЁЩҗШ§Щ„Щ’ШӯЩҺЩӮЩ‘Щҗ** вҖ” *Say, вҖҳThe Holy Spirit has brought it down from your Lord with the truthвҖҷ (an-Nahl: 102).* Discussion regarding the meaning of the Spirit (Ш§Щ„ШұЩҲШӯ) has already preceded in the commentary (ШӘЩҒШіЩҠШұ) of Surah an-Nahl and Surah al-Isra.

The Spirit was described as "Trustworthy" (Ш§Щ„ШЈЩ…ЩҠЩҶ) to indicate that he is secure in his mission from Him, the Exalted, to His Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ; he does not change anything of the speech of the Exalted through substitution (ШӘШЁШҜЩҠЩ„) or distortion (ШӘШӯШұЩҠЩҒ), whether intentionally, by mistake, or through forgetfulness, just as his description in another verse as "Holy" (Ш§Щ„ЩӮШҜШі) points to this.

His saying: **ЩҶЩҺШІЩҺЩ„ЩҺ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШұЩ‘ЩҸЩҲШӯЩҸ** вҖ” the preposition *bДҒвҖҷ* (ШЁ) is for transitivity (Ш§Щ„ШӘШ№ШҜЩҠШ©), meaning "the Trustworthy Spirit brought it down." As for the opinion of those who say the *bДҒвҖҷ* is for accompaniment (Ш§Щ„Щ…ШөШ§ШӯШЁШ©), meaning "the Spirit descended with it," it is not to be considered, because the emphasis in this context is on the descent of the Quran, not the descent of the Spirit alongside the Quran.

The pronoun in **ЩҶЩҺШІЩҺЩ„ЩҺ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ** refers to the Quran as speech composed of words that possess true meanings. Indeed, the words of the Quran are descended from Him, the Exalted, just as its meanings are descended from Him, as is apparent from His saying: **ЩҒЩҺШҘЩҗШ°Ш§ ЩӮЩҺШұЩҺШЈЩ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ ЩҒЩҺШ§ШӘЩ‘ЩҺШЁЩҗШ№Щ’ ЩӮЩҸШұЩ’ШўЩҶЩҺЩҮЩҸ** вҖ” *So when We have recited it, follow its recitation (al-Qiyamah: 18)*, and His saying: **ШӘЩҗЩ„Щ’ЩғЩҺ ШўЩҠШ§ШӘЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ ЩҶЩҺШӘЩ’Щ„ЩҸЩҲЩҮШ§ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩғЩҺ ШЁЩҗШ§Щ„Щ’ШӯЩҺЩӮЩ‘Щҗ** вҖ” *These are the signs of Allah which We recite to you with the truth (Al вҖҳImran: 108, al-Jathiyah: 6)*, among others.

Therefore, no weight is given to the claim of those who say that what the Trustworthy Spirit brought down were only the meanings of the Noble Quran, and then the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ expressed them with words corresponding to and narrating them in an Arabic tongue.

Even more absurd is the claim of those who say that the Quran, in both its wording and meaning, is among the productions of the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ, projected from a level of his noble soul (ЩҶЩҒШі) called the Trustworthy Spirit to another level of it called the heart (Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩ„ШЁ).

What is meant by the "heart" (Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩ„ШЁ) to which perception (Ш§Щ„ШҘШҜШұШ§Щғ) and consciousness (Ш§Щ„ШҙШ№ЩҲШұ) are attributed in His speech, the Exalted, is the human soul (Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҒШі Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҶШіШ§ЩҶЩҠШ©) which possesses perception and to which all types of consciousness and will (Ш§Щ„ШҘШұШ§ШҜШ©) terminate, rather than the pineal-shaped flesh suspended on the left side of the chest, which is one of the primary organs. This is understood from various places in His speech, the Exalted, such as His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШЁЩҺЩ„ЩҺШәЩҺШӘЩҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩӮЩҸЩ„ЩҸЩҲШЁЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШӯЩҺЩҶШ§Ш¬ЩҗШұЩҺ** вҖ” *...and the hearts reached the throats (al-Ahzab: 10)*, meaning the spirits (Ш§Щ„ШЈШұЩҲШ§Шӯ); and His saying: **ЩҒЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸ ШўШ«ЩҗЩ…ЩҢ ЩӮЩҺЩ„Щ’ШЁЩҸЩҮЩҸ** вҖ” *...his heart is indeed sinful (al-Baqarah: 283)*, meaning his soul, as there is no meaning in attributing sin to a specific physical organ.

Perhaps the reason for His saying: **ЩҶЩҺШІЩҺЩ„ЩҺ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШұЩ‘ЩҸЩҲШӯЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү ЩӮЩҺЩ„Щ’ШЁЩҗЩғЩҺ** вҖ” *The Trustworthy Spirit has brought it down to your heart* вҖ” rather than saying "upon you" (Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩғ), is to point to the manner in which he ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ received the Quran revealed to him; that what received it from the Spirit was his noble soul, without the participation of the external senses (Ш§Щ„ШӯЩҲШ§Ші Ш§Щ„ШёШ§ЩҮШұШ©) which are the tools used for perceiving particular matters (Ш§Щ„ШЈЩ…ЩҲШұ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ШІШҰЩҠШ©).

Thus, he ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ would see and hear while the revelation (Ш§Щ„ЩҲШӯЩҠ) was being given to him without using the senses of sight and hearing, as it is narrated that he would be overtaken by a state resembling a faint, called the "heaviness of revelation" (ШЁШұШ¬Ш§ШЎ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШӯЩҠ).

He ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ would see the person and hear the voice just as we see a person and hear a voice, except that he did not use his material senses of sight and hearing for that as we use them.

If his seeing and hearing were through material sight and hearing, then what he experienced would have been shared between him and others; other people would have seen what he saw and heard what he heard. Definitive transmission (Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩӮЩ„ Ш§Щ„ЩӮШ·Ш№ЩҠ) refutes this, for he was often overtaken by the state of revelation while among people, and revelation would be given to him while those around him perceived nothing, nor did they witness any person speaking to him or any speech being delivered to him.

The claim that it is possible for Allah, the Exalted, to divert the senses of others from some of what his ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ senses were perceivingвҖ”namely the matters of the Unseen (Ш§Щ„ШЈЩ…ЩҲШұ Ш§Щ„ШәЩҠШЁЩҠШ©) hidden from usвҖ”is a destruction of the foundation of scientific affirmation (Ш§Щ„ШӘШөШҜЩҠЩӮ Ш§Щ„Ш№Щ„Щ…ЩҠ). For if such a grave error were possible regarding the senses, which are the key to necessary knowledge (Ш§Щ„Ш№Щ„ЩҲЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш¶ШұЩҲШұЩҠШ©) and self-evident affirmations (Ш§Щ„ШӘШөШҜЩҠЩӮШ§ШӘ Ш§Щ„ШЁШҜЩҠЩҮЩҠШ©) and others, no trust would remain in any knowledge or affirmation.

Furthermore, this argument is based on the primacy of the senses (ШЈШөШ§Щ„Ш© Ш§Щ„ШӯШі) and the notion that nothing exists except the sensible (Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӯШіЩҲШі), which is a most egregious error. Discussion regarding the meaning of the angel's manifestation (ШӘЩ…Ш«Щ„ Ш§Щ„Щ…Щ„Щғ) has already preceded in the commentary of Surah Maryam, which is relevant here.

It has been said that the reason for specifying the heart for the descent is that it is the perceiving and legally responsible (Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩғЩ„ЩҒ) entity rather than the body, even if it receives the revelation through the mediation of bodily tools like hearing and sight; you have already seen the flaw in this.

It has also been said: Since the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ had two aspectsвҖ”an angelic aspect (Ш¬ЩҮШ© Щ…Щ„ЩғЩҠШ©) through which he receives emanation, and a human aspect (Ш¬ЩҮШ© ШЁШҙШұЩҠШ©) through which he provides emanationвҖ”the descent was placed upon his spirit because it is what is characterized by the angelic attributes through which he receives from the Trustworthy Spirit. To point to this, it was said: **Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү ЩӮЩҺЩ„Щ’ШЁЩҗЩғЩҺ** (to your heart) and it did not say "upon you," despite the latter being more concise. [End of quote].

This too is based on the participation of the senses and bodily faculties in receiving revelation, so the same refutation we provided earlier applies to it.

A group of commentators (Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҒШіШұЩҠЩҶ) mentioned that what is meant by the heart is the specific physical organ, and that perception, in whatever form, is one of its properties.

Among them are those who said: Making the heart the object of the descent is based on figurative extension (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҲШіШ№), because Allah, the Exalted, makes Gabriel hear the Quran by creating the sound, so he memorizes it and descends with it to the Messenger ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ and recites it to him, so he comprehends it and preserves it in his heart; thus, it is as if it descended upon his heart.

Among them are those who said: The specification of the heart for the descent is because spiritual meanings (Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш№Ш§ЩҶЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШұЩҲШӯШ§ЩҶЩҠШ©) descend first upon the spirit, then transfer from it to the heart due to the connection between them, then transfer from it to the brain, where they are inscribed upon the tablet of the imagination (Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӘШ®ЩҠЩ„Ш©).

Among them are those who said: Its specification is to point to the perfection of his ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ intellection (ШӘШ№ЩӮЩ„ЩҮ), as he did not require intermediaries like hearing, sight, and others.

Among them are those who said: This is to point to the righteousness and sanctity of his ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ heart, as it was the dwelling place for the speech of the Exalted, so that the righteousness of the rest of his parts and limbs might be known through it; for the heart is the chief of all limbs and their king, and if the king is righteous, the subjects are righteous.

Among them are those who said: This is because Allah, the Exalted, granted the heart of His Messenger ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ a specific hearing and sight through which he hears and sees, distinguishing his status from others, as suggested by His saying, the Exalted: **Щ…Ш§ ЩғЩҺШ°ЩҺШЁЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩҒЩҸШӨШ§ШҜЩҸ Щ…Ш§ ШұЩҺШЈЩү** вҖ” *The heart did not feign what it saw (an-Najm: 11).*

These views, in addition to most of them containing conjecture, are based on analogizing these matters of the Unseen to the material events we have and applying their rules to them. The arbitrariness of some reached the point of saying: The meaning of the angel bringing down the Quran is that Allah inspired him with His speech while he was in heaven and taught him its recitation, then the angel delivered it on earth while descending in space. In this, there are two methods: one is that the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ divested himself of the human form into the angelic form and took it from the angel; the second is that the angel divested himself into the human form so that the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ could take it from him, and the first is the more difficult of the two states. [End of quote].

I wonder what he imagined regarding a human divesting himself of his form into an angelic form and becoming an angel, then returning to being a human, and an angel divesting himself into a human form, when he has assumed for each of them an identity (ЩҮЩҲЩҠШ©) distinct from the other, with no link between them in essence (Ш°Ш§ШӘШ§) or effect (ШЈШ«ШұШ§). There are other points in his speech for consideration that are not hidden from one who reflects upon it.

There is a completion to this research; perhaps Allah, the Glorified, will grant us the success to fulfill it by providing a comprehensive discourse on the angel (Ш§Щ„Щ…Щ„Щғ) and another on revelation (Ш§Щ„ЩҲШӯЩҠ).

His saying: **Щ„ЩҗШӘЩҺЩғЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...so that you may be one of the warners (194)*; meaning those who call toward Allah, the Glorified, by frightening people with His punishment. This is what is meant by "warning" (Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҶШ°Ш§Шұ) in the parlance of the Quran, rather than the Prophet or Messenger specifically. The Exalted said regarding the believers among the Jinn: **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗШ°Щ’ ШөЩҺШұЩҺЩҒЩ’ЩҶШ§ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩғЩҺ ЩҶЩҺЩҒЩҺШұШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш¬ЩҗЩҶЩ‘Щҗ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’ШӘЩҺЩ…ЩҗШ№ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩӮЩҸШұЩ’ШўЩҶЩҺ ЩҒЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ШӯЩҺШ¶ЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҮЩҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҸЩҲШ§ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШөЩҗШӘЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩӮЩҸШ¶ЩҗЩҠЩҺ ЩҲЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҲЩ’Ш§ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щү ЩӮЩҺЩҲЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *When We stepped a group of jinn toward you to listen to the Quran, when they attended it, they said, вҖҳListen!вҖҷ When it was finished, they returned to their people as warners (al-Ahqaf: 29).* And He said regarding those who seek understanding among the believers: **Щ„ЩҗЩҠЩҺШӘЩҺЩҒЩҺЩӮЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШҜЩ‘ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҗ ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩҸЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩӮЩҺЩҲЩ’Щ…ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШҘЩҗШ°Ш§ ШұЩҺШ¬ЩҺШ№ЩҸЩҲШ§ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’** вҖ” *...so that they may acquire a deep understanding of religion, and warn their people when they return to them (at-Tawbah: 122).*

His ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ warning was mentioned as the goal of the Quran's descent, rather than his prophethood or messengership, because the context of the Surah's verses is one of frightening and threatening.

His saying: **ШЁЩҗЩ„ЩҗШіШ§ЩҶЩҚ Ш№ЩҺШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩҚ Щ…ЩҸШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ** вҖ” *...in a clear Arabic language (195)*; meaning manifest in its Arabicity or clarifying the objectives with complete clarity. The prepositional phrase is connected to "brought down" (ЩҶШІЩ„), meaning: He sent it down in a clear Arabic language.

Some have allowed it to be connected to His saying: **Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (the warners), meaning: He sent it down to your heart so that you may enter the group of prophets from the Arabs; among those mentioned in the Quran are Hud, Salih, Ishmael, and Shu'ayb (peace be upon them). The first of the two views is the better one.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸ Щ„ЩҺЩҒЩҗЩҠ ШІЩҸШЁЩҸШұЩҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩҲЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *And indeed it is in the scriptures of the ancients (196).* The pronoun refers to the Quran or its descent upon the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ. The term **ШІШЁШұ** (scriptures) is the plural of *zabЕ«r*, which is a book. The meaning is: Indeed, the news of the Quran or the news of its descent upon you is in the books of the past prophets.

It has been said: The pronoun refers to the universal gnoses (Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш№Ш§ШұЩҒ Ш§Щ„ЩғЩ„ЩҠШ©) within the Quran; meaning that the Quranic gnoses are present and mentioned in the books of the past prophets.

Against this: Firstly, the polytheists did not believe in the prophets and their books such that they could be argued against by what is in them regarding monotheism, the Resurrection (Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш№Ш§ШҜ), and others. This is unlike the mention of the news of the Quran and its descent upon the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ in the books of the ancients, for it would then be a prophecy (Щ…Щ„ШӯЩ…Ш©) that souls are compelled to accept.

Secondly, it does not harmonize with the following verse.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ШЈЩҺЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…Щ’ ЩҠЩҺЩғЩҸЩҶЩ’ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШўЩҠЩҺШ©ЩӢ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҮЩҸ Ш№ЩҸЩ„ЩҺЩ…Ш§ШЎЩҸ ШЁЩҺЩҶЩҗЩҠ ШҘЩҗШіЩ’ШұШ§ШҰЩҗЩҠЩ„ЩҺ** вҖ” *Is it not a sign for them that the learned of the Children of Israel know it? (197).* The pronoun in **ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҮЩҸ** (that they know it) refers to the news of the Quran or the news of its descent upon the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ. That is: Was not the knowledge of the scholars of the Children of Israel regarding the news of the Quran or its descent upon youвҖ”by way of glad tidings in the books of the past prophetsвҖ”a sign for the polytheists of the truth of your prophethood? The Jews used to give glad tidings of this and seek victory over the Arabs through it, as mentioned in His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ЩӮЩҺШЁЩ’Щ„ЩҸ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’ШӘЩҺЩҒЩ’ШӘЩҗШӯЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩү Ш§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ЩғЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§** вҖ” *...though before that they used to pray for victory over the faithless (al-Baqarah: 89).*

A number of Jewish scholars embraced Islam during the time of the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ and admitted that he was prophesied in their books. This Surah is among the early Meccan Surahs revealed before the Migration (Ш§Щ„ЩҮШ¬ШұШ©), and the enmity of the Jews toward the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ had not reached the level it did after the Migration; it was hoped that they would speak some of the truth they possessed, even in a general way.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ ЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү ШЁЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¶Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ЩҒЩҺЩӮЩҺШұЩҺШЈЩҺЩҮЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ Щ…ЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *Had We sent it down to some of the non-Arabs, and had he recited it to them, they would not have believed in it (198-199).* It is said in *al-Mufradat*: "Non-Arabicity (Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш¬Щ…Ш©) is the opposite of clarity (Ш§Щ„ШҘШЁШ§ЩҶШ©), and *iвҖҳjДҒm* is ambiguity (Ш§Щ„ШҘШЁЩҮШ§Щ…)..." until he said: "The *вҖҳAjam* (Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш¬Щ…) are the opposite of the Arabs, and the *вҖҳAjamД«* is attributed to them. The *AвҖҳjam* (Ш§Щ„ШЈШ№Ш¬Щ…) is one in whose tongue there is non-Arabicity, whether he is an Arab or otherwise, considering their lack of understanding from the non-Arabs; from this, the beast is called *вҖҳajmДҒвҖҷ*. The *AвҖҳjamД«* is attributed to him. His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ ЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү ШЁЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¶Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” is with the deletion of the *yДҒвҖҷ*s." [End of quote].

What he mentioned impliesвҖ”as you seeвҖ”that the origin of *al-AвҖҳjamД«n* is *al-AвҖҳjamiyyД«n*, then the *yДҒвҖҷ* of attribution was deleted; others have stated this explicitly. Some mentioned that the reason is that *aвҖҳjam* has the feminine *вҖҳajmДҒвҖҷ*, and the form *afвҖҳal faвҖҳlДҒвҖҷ* does not take the sound plural, but the Kufan grammarians allow this. The apparent wording supports their view, so there is no reason to claim a deletion.

In any case, the apparent context connects these two verses to His saying: **ШЁЩҗЩ„ЩҗШіШ§ЩҶЩҚ Ш№ЩҺШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩҚ Щ…ЩҸШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ** (in a clear Arabic language). Thus, they are in the position of providing the rationale for it. The meaning would be: We sent it down to you in an Arabic tongue of manifest Arabicity and clear indication so that they might believe in it and not make excuses based on their lack of understanding of its objectives. Had We sent it down to some of the non-Arabs in a non-Arabic tongue, they would not have believed in it and would have rejected it due to not understanding its objectives.

Thus, the meaning of its descent upon some of the non-Arabs would be its descent in a non-Arabic language and in his tongue. These two verses and the one following them carry the same meaning as His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ Ш¬ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ ЩӮЩҸШұЩ’ШўЩҶШ§ЩӢ ШЈЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩӢШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ Щ„Ш§ ЩҒЩҸШөЩ‘ЩҗЩ„ЩҺШӘЩ’ ШўЩҠШ§ШӘЩҸЩҮЩҸШЎЩҺ ШЈЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩҢ ЩҲЩҺШ№ЩҺШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩҢ ЩӮЩҸЩ„Щ’ ЩҮЩҸЩҲЩҺ Щ„ЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШўЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҮЩҸШҜЩүЩӢ ЩҲЩҺШҙЩҗЩҒШ§ШЎЩҢ ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ ШўШ°Ш§ЩҶЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ ЩҲЩҺЩӮЩ’ШұЩҢ ЩҲЩҺЩҮЩҸЩҲЩҺ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ…ЩӢЩү** вҖ” *Had We made it a non-Arabic Quran, they would have said, вҖҳWhy are its signs not articulated? [What! A] non-Arabic [revelation] and an Arab [messenger]?вҖҷ Say, вҖҳIt is guidance and a cure for those who have faith, and as for those who do not believe, there is a deafness in their ears and it is a blindness for themвҖҷ (Fussilat: 44).*

Some have said: The meaning is that had We sent it down as an Arabic Quran as it is, with its exquisite and miraculous composition, to some of the non-Arabs who are unable to speak Arabic, and he recited it to them with a correct, miraculous recitation, they would not have believed in itвҖ”despite the joining of the miracle of recitation to the miracle of what is recitedвҖ”due to the extremity of their stubbornness and the intensity of their persistence in obstinacy.

He said: As for the saying of some that the meaning is "had We sent it down to some of the non-Arabs in the language of the non-Arabs and he recited it to them, they would not have believed in it," it is not quite right, for it is far from being suitable for the context of explaining their persistence in obstinacy and stubbornness. [End of summary].

Against this: The connection of the two verses to His saying: **ШЁЩҗЩ„ЩҗШіШ§ЩҶЩҚ Ш№ЩҺШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩҚ Щ…ЩҸШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ** is closer to them than their connection to the context of the disbelievers' persistence in their disbelief and denial; you have already seen the clarification of this.

It could be objected to the previous view that the pronoun in His saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ ЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү ШЁЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¶Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** refers to *this* Quran which is Arabic. So if the meaning were its descent in a non-Arabic tongue, the meaning would be: "Had We sent down the Arabic as non-Arabic," which is nonsensical.

This is refuted by the fact that it is like His saying, the Exalted: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Ш¬ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ ЩӮЩҸШұЩ’ШўЩҶШ§ЩӢ Ш№ЩҺШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩӢШ§ Щ„ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҺЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’ЩӮЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *We have made it an Arabic Quran so that you may exercise your reason (az-Zukhruf: 3).* There is no meaning in our saying: "We made the Arabic Arabic." Thus, the "Quran" in any case refers to the recited Book.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩғЩҺШ°Щ„ЩҗЩғЩҺ ШіЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩғЩ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ ЩӮЩҸЩ„ЩҸЩҲШЁЩҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШ¬Щ’ШұЩҗЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *Thus do We let it pass through the hearts of the guilty (200).* The indication in His saying **ЩғЩҺШ°Щ„ЩҗЩғЩҺ** (thus) is to the state the Quran is in with the polytheists, which was mentioned in the preceding verses: that they disregard it and do not believe in it, even though it is a revelation from the Lord of all the worlds, is in clear Arabic and not non-Arabic, and was mentioned in the scriptures of the ancients and known by the scholars of the Children of Israel.

**Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„ЩҲЩғ** (passing through) means entering into a path and causing to pass. What is meant by "the guilty" (Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш¬ШұЩ…ЩҠЩҶ) are the disbelievers and polytheists. They were mentioned with the attribute of guilt (Ш§Щ„ШҘШ¬ШұШ§Щ…) to point to the cause of the judgmentвҖ”which is its passing through their hearts in this hated and repelled stateвҖ”and that this is a divine recompense with which He repaid them for their guilt, and so that the judgment may be general due to the generality of the cause.

The meaning is: In this stateвҖ”which is such that it is disregarded and not believed inвҖ”We enter the Quran into the hearts of these polytheists and cause it to pass through their souls as a recompense for their guilt; and likewise for every guilty person.

It has been said: The indication is to the noble attributes of the Quran mentioned. The meaning would be: We enter the Quran and cause it to pass through the hearts of the guilty just as We have explained its attributes, so they see that it is a heavenly book with a miraculous composition beyond human power, and that it was prophesied in the scriptures of the ancients and known by the scholars of the Children of Israel, and the proof (Ш§Щ„ШӯШ¬Ш©) is completed against them through it. This is far from the context.

It has also been said: The pronoun in **ШіЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩғЩ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ** refers to the denial of the Quran and disbelief in it, as indicated by His saying: **Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ Щ…ЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (they would not have believed in it). This is close to the first view, but the first view is more elegant and precise, and it was mentioned in *al-Kashshaf*.

It has become clear from what preceded that what is meant by "the guilty" are the polytheists of Mecca, except that the generality of the attribute of guilt generalizes the judgment. Some said: What is meant by "the guilty" are those other than the polytheists of Mecca among their contemporaries and those who come after them. The meaning would be: Just as We let it pass through the hearts of the polytheists of Mecca, We let it pass through the hearts of other guilty ones.

Perhaps what led him to choose this view was the problem of the identity of the thing likened (Ш§Щ„Щ…ШҙШЁЩҮ) and the thing likened to (Ш§Щ„Щ…ШҙШЁЩҮ ШЁЩҮ) in the first view, while there must be a distinction between them. So he considered the referent of **ЩғЩҺШ°Щ„ЩҗЩғЩҺ** to be the passing through the hearts of the polytheists of MeccaвҖ”which is the thing likened toвҖ”and made the thing likened to be other guilty ones. Against this: Likening a universal (Ш§Щ„ЩғЩ„ЩҠ) to some of its particulars (ШЈЩҒШұШ§ШҜЩҮ) to indicate the pervasiveness of its judgment in all particulars is a common method.

From here it appears that there is another view: that what is meant by "the guilty" is what encompasses the polytheists of Mecca and others, by making the definite article *al-* (Ш§Щ„) for other than specific reference (Ш§Щ„Ш№ЩҮШҜ). Perhaps the first view is closer to the context.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩү ЩҠЩҺШұЩҺЩҲЩҸШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺШ°Ш§ШЁЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҺ** вҖ” *They will not believe in it until they sight the painful punishment (201)* вҖ” until His saying вҖ” **Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’ШёЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (given any respite). This is an explanation and clarification of His saying: **ЩғЩҺШ°Щ„ЩҗЩғЩҺ ШіЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩғЩ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ** etc. This is according to the first and third views mentioned regarding the previous verse; as for the second view, it is a new sentence (Ш§ШіШӘШҰЩҶШ§ЩҒ) unrelated to what precedes it.

His saying: **ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩү ЩҠЩҺШұЩҺЩҲЩҸШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺШ°Ш§ШЁЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҺ** вҖ” meaning until they witness the painful punishment, which will then force them into a compelled faith (Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҠЩ…Ш§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш§Ш¶Ш·ШұШ§ШұЩҠ) that will not benefit them. It is apparent that what is meant by the painful punishment is what they witness at the time of death. Some considered it possible that what is meant is what befell them on the day of Badr of being killed, but the generality of the judgment in the previous verse for the polytheists of Mecca and others does not harmonize with that.

His saying: **ЩҒЩҺЩҠЩҺШЈЩ’ШӘЩҗЩҠЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШЁЩҺШәЩ’ШӘЩҺШ©ЩӢ ЩҲЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҺШҙЩ’Ш№ЩҸШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...so that it will come upon them suddenly while they are unaware (202)* вҖ” is like an explanation for His saying: **ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩү ЩҠЩҺШұЩҺЩҲЩҸШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺШ°Ш§ШЁЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҺ**. For if it did not come upon them suddenly and they knew of it before its appointed time, they would have prepared for it and believed by their own choice without being compelled.

His saying: **ЩҒЩҺЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҮЩҺЩ„Щ’ ЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’ШёЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...and they will say, вҖҳShall we be given any respite?вҖҷ (203)* вҖ” is a word of regret from them.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺШЁЩҗШ№ЩҺШ°Ш§ШЁЩҗЩҶШ§ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *Do they seek to hasten Our punishment? (204)* вҖ” is a rebuke and a threat.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҺШЈЩҺЩҠЩ’ШӘЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ Щ…ЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШ№Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШіЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *Tell Me, if We let them enjoy for some years (205)* вҖ” until His saying вҖ” **ЩҠЩҸЩ…ЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШ№ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (they were given to enjoy). This is connected to His saying: **ЩҒЩҺЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҮЩҺЩ„Щ’ ЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’ШёЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ**. The sum of the meaning is that the wish for delay and respite is a wish for something that would not benefit them if it occurred as they wished, and would not avail them anything if they were granted what they asked. For their being given enjoyment for a limited period, whether long or short, does not remove the eternal punishment that has been decreed against them.

This is His saying: **ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҺШЈЩҺЩҠЩ’ШӘЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ Щ…ЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШ№Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШіЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” a numbered few that will end; **Ш«ЩҸЩ…Щ‘ЩҺ Ш¬Ш§ШЎЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩҸЩҲШ№ЩҺШҜЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...then there comes to them what they were promised (206)* вҖ” of punishment after the passing of the years of respite and delay; **Щ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺШәЩ’ЩҶЩү Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩҸЩ…ЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШ№ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...what they were given to enjoy will not avail them (207)* вҖ” meaning their enjoyment for a limited period.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺЩҮЩ’Щ„ЩҺЩғЩ’ЩҶШ§ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ЩӮЩҺШұЩ’ЩҠЩҺШ©ЩҚ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Ш°ЩҗЩғЩ’ШұЩү** вҖ” *We have not destroyed any town but it had warners [to provide it] with a reminder (208-209)* etc. It is most likely that His saying **Щ„ЩҺЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** is a circumstantial clause (ШӯШ§Щ„) for "town" (ЩӮШұЩҠШ©), and His saying **Ш°ЩҗЩғЩ’ШұЩү** (a reminder) is a circumstantial clause for the plural pronoun in "warners" (Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ), or an absolute object (Щ…ЩҒШ№ЩҲЩ„ Щ…Ш·Щ„ЩӮ) whose governing verb is "warners" because it carries the meaning of "reminders." The meaning is manifest. Other things have been said which are of no use to mention or prolong the discussion over.

His saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ЩғЩҸЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ ШёШ§Щ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...and We were not unjust (209).* The entry of the negation upon the state of being (Ш§Щ„ЩғЩҲЩҶ) rather than saying "and We did not wrong them" or the like, expresses the negation of the very nature of the act (ЩҶЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШҙШЈЩҶЩҠШ©); meaning: it was not of Our nature, nor to be expected of Us, that We should wrong them.

The sentence is in the position of providing the rationale for the preceding restriction. The meaning is: We did not destroy any town except in a state where it had warners and reminders through whom the proof was completed against them; for if We had destroyed them in other than this state, We would have been unjust to them, and it is not of Our nature to wrong anyone. Thus, the verse carries the same meaning as His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ЩғЩҸЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ…ЩҸШ№ЩҺШ°Щ‘ЩҗШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩү ЩҶЩҺШЁЩ’Ш№ЩҺШ«ЩҺ ШұЩҺШіЩҸЩҲЩ„Ш§ЩӢ** вҖ” *...and We do not punish until We have sent an apostle (al-Isra: 15).*

## A Discourse on the Meaning of the Negation of Injustice (Ш§Щ„ШёЩ„Щ…) from Him, the Exalted

Among the necessary concomitants of the meaning of injustice (Ш§Щ„ШёЩ„Щ…) вҖ” which are equivalent to it вҖ” is the agent's act and his disposal of that which he does not own in terms of action and disposal. Its opposite is justice (Ш§Щ„Ш№ШҜЩ„), and its concomitant is that it is the agent's act and his disposal of that which he owns.

From here it appears that the acts of ontological agents (Ш§Щ„ЩҒЩҲШ§Ш№Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШӘЩғЩҲЩҠЩҶЩҠШ©), insofar as they are owned by them ontologically (ШӘЩғЩҲЩҶЩҠШ§), do not manifest the meaning of injustice. This is because the assumption of an act's issuance from its agent ontologically is synonymous with its being owned by him, in the sense that its existence subsists through him in a manner that it has no independence without him.

For Allah, the Glorified, there is absolute ownership (Щ…Щ„Щғ Щ…Ш·Щ„ЩӮ) extended over all things from all aspects of their existence, because they subsist through Him, the Exalted, without any self-sufficiency from Him or independence without Him. Thus, any disposal He makes in themвҖ”whether it pleases them or grieves them, benefits them or harms themвҖ”is not in any way part of injustice. Or if you prefer, say: it is justice in the sense of what is not injustice. He has the right to do what He wills and to judge as He intends, all of that according to the ontological order (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩғЩҲЩҠЩҶ).

Thus, He, the Exalted, possesses absolute ownership by His essence (ШЁШ°Ш§ШӘЩҮ), and other ontological agents possess an ontological ownership relative to their acts according to the divine bestowal and gift. This is an ownership in the longitudinal extension (ЩҒЩҠ Ш·ЩҲЩ„) of His ownership, the Exalted; He is the Owner of what He has made them own, and the Controller over that which He has given them authority.

Among these agents is the human species in relation to its acts, especially those we call voluntary acts (Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҒШ№Ш§Щ„ Ш§Щ„Ш§Ш®ШӘЩҠШ§ШұЩҠШ©) and the choice (Ш§Щ„Ш§Ш®ШӘЩҠШ§Шұ) by which these acts are determined. Each one of us finds within himself through direct witnessing that he owns choice, in the sense of the possibility of both acting and refraining. If he wills, he acts; and if he does not will, he refrains. He sees himself as free, owning the act and the refrainingвҖ”whatever act or refraining they may beвҖ”in the sense of the possibility of either issuing from him.

Furthermore, the human necessity for social, civil life compelled the intellect (Ш§Щ„Ш№ЩӮЩ„) to overlook some of the freedom of action that the human possesses, and to withdraw the hand from some acts that he saw himself as owningвҖ”namely those whose performance would disrupt the order of society, thereby disrupting the order of his own life. These are the prohibitions (Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӯШұЩ…Ш§ШӘ) and sins (Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш№Ш§ШөЩҠ) that are forbidden by civil laws, national customs, or the royal decrees prevalent in societies.

It is necessary for the enforcement of these laws and customs that some type of negative recompense (Ш§Щ„Ш¬ШІШ§ШЎ Ш§Щ„ШіЩҠШҰ) be established for those who violate themвҖ”on the condition of knowledge and the completion of the proof, as it is a condition for the realization of legal obligation (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩғЩ„ЩҠЩҒ)вҖ”such as blame or punishment; and a type of beautiful reward (Ш§Щ„ШЈШ¬Шұ Ш§Щ„Ш¬Щ…ЩҠЩ„) for the obedient who respects them, such as praise or reward.

It is also necessary that there be someone appointed over the society and the laws current within it to enforce them as they are, and he is responsible for what he was appointed for, especially regarding the laws of recompense. For if he were not responsible, and it were permissible for him to recompense or not to recompense, and to take the doer of good while leaving the doer of evil, the establishment of laws and customs would be futile from the start. These are rational principles (ШЈШөЩҲЩ„ Ш№ЩӮЩ„Ш§ШҰЩҠШ©) current generally in human societies since this species settled on the earth, originating from their human primordial nature (Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ·ШұШ©).

Rational proofs have indicated, and the successive reports (ШӘЩҲШ§ШӘШұ) of the prophets and messengers from Him, the Exalted, have supported, that social laws and the customs of life must be from Him, the Exalted. These are human judgments and duties to which the human primordial nature (Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ·ШұШ© Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҶШіШ§ЩҶЩҠШ©) вҖ” *the innate disposition toward truth that Allah placed in every human being* вҖ” guides, and which guarantee the happiness of his life and preserve the interests of his society.

This heavenly, primordial law (Ш§Щ„ШҙШұЩҠШ№Ш© Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…Ш§ЩҲЩҠШ© Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ·ШұЩҠШ©), its Legislator is Allah, the Glorified, and its Enforcer in terms of reward and punishmentвҖ”and their place is the place of return to Him, the ExaltedвҖ”is Allah, the Glorified.

The requirement of His legislating these heavenly laws and His considering Himself their Enforcer is that He has made it obligatory upon Himself by a legislative obligation (ШҘЩҠШ¬Ш§ШЁШ§ ШӘШҙШұЩҠШ№ЩҠШ§)вҖ”not an ontological oneвҖ”that He shall not contradict Himself, nor fail by neglecting or canceling a recompense necessitated by a violation, or implementing a recompense not deserved by an act, such as punishing the heedless and ignorant with the punishment of the intentional and stubborn, or taking the wronged for the sin of the wrongdoer. Otherwise, it would be injustice (ШёЩ„Щ…) from Him; Exalted is He far above that.

Perhaps this is the meaning of what is said: that injustice is within His power, the Exalted, but it never occurs, because it is a deficiency in perfection from which He, the Exalted, is transcendent. Thus, the assumption of injustice from Him, the Exalted, is the assumption of the impossible (Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӯШ§Щ„), but it is not an impossible assumption (Щ„ЩҠШі ШЁЩҒШұШ¶ Щ…ШӯШ§Щ„). This is what is understood from the apparent meaning of His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ЩғЩҸЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ ШёШ§Щ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (and We were not unjust), and His saying in verse 209 of the Surah: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҺ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҺШёЩ’Щ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ШіЩҺ ШҙЩҺЩҠЩ’ШҰШ§ЩӢ** вҖ” *Indeed Allah does not wrong people in the least (Yunus: 44)*, and His saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҸЩғЩҺ ШЁЩҗШёЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§Щ…ЩҚ Щ„ЩҗЩ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠШҜЩҗ** вҖ” *...and your Lord is not at all unjust to [His] servants (Fussilat: 46)*, and His saying: **Щ„ЩҗШҰЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺЩғЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ„ЩҗЩ„ЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ШіЩҗ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ ШӯЩҸШ¬Щ‘ЩҺШ©ЩҢ ШЁЩҺШ№Щ’ШҜЩҺ Ш§Щ„ШұЩ‘ЩҸШіЩҸЩ„Щҗ** вҖ” *...so that the people may not have any argument against Allah after the apostles (an-Nisa: 165).* Their apparent meaning is that they are not of the type of a negative proposition due to the non-existence of the subject (Ш§Щ„ШіШ§Щ„ШЁШ© ШЁШ§ЩҶШӘЩҒШ§ШЎ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№), as suggested by the commentary of those who explained them as meaning that Allah does not perform an act which, if performed by someone else, would be injustice.

If you say: What was mentioned regarding the necessity of enforcing recompense as reward or punishment contradicts what is accepted among them: that leaving the punishment of the sinner is permissible because it is the right of the punisher, and it is permissible for the possessor of a right to leave it and not demand it; unlike the reward of the obedient, because it is the right of anotherвҖ”namely the obedientвҖ”so it is not permissible to leave it or invalidate it.

Furthermore, it has been said: Rewarding for acts of obedience is from grace (Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ¶Щ„) rather than entitlement (Ш§Щ„Ш§ШіШӘШӯЩӮШ§ЩӮ), because the servant and his work belong to his Master, so he owns nothing such that he could exchange it for something.

I say: Leaving the punishment of the sinner in some cases (ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¬Щ…Щ„Ш©) is not disputed, because it is from grace. But as for doing so entirely (ШЁШ§Щ„Ш¬Щ…Щ„Ш©), then no, because it would necessitate the futility of legislation, law-making, and the arrangement of recompense upon action.

As for the reward of deeds being from graceвҖ”considering that the servant's work, like himself, belongs to AllahвҖ”this does not contradict another grace from Him, the Exalted, toward His servant by considering his work as his own property, then making what He rewards him with an "upholding" (ШЈШ¬Шұ) for his work. The Quran is full of the mention of the reward (Ш§Щ„ШЈШ¬Шұ) for righteous deeds (Ш§Щ„ШЈШ№Щ…Ш§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШөШ§Щ„ШӯШ©), and He, the Exalted, has said: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҺ Ш§ШҙЩ’ШӘЩҺШұЩү Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҒЩҸШіЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’ЩҲШ§Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШЁЩҗШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш¬ЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ©ЩҺ** вҖ” *Indeed Allah has bought from the faithful their souls and their possessions for paradise to be theirs (at-Tawbah: 111).*

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҺШӘЩ’ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҺЩҠШ§Ш·ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҸ** вҖ” *The devils have not brought it down (210)* вҖ” until His saying вҖ” **Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ№Щ’ШІЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (are indeed barred). This is the beginning of the response to the polytheists' claim that Muhammad has a jinn who brings him this speech, and their claim that he is a poet. The response to the first was presented first. The speech was first directed to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ, explaining to him that the Quran is not from the descent of the devils, thereby comforting his soul; then the speech was directed to the people, explaining it to them in a way they could comprehend.

So His saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҺШӘЩ’ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҺЩҠШ§Ш·ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҸ** вҖ” meaning they did not bring it down. The verse is connected to His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸ Щ„ЩҺШӘЩҺЩҶЩ’ШІЩҗЩҠЩ„ЩҸ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№Ш§Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (And indeed, it is a revelation from the Lord of all the worlds). The speech is directed, as you heard, to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ, as evidenced by His saying immediately after: **ЩҒЩҺЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩҺШҜЩ’Ш№ЩҸ Щ…ЩҺШ№ЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҮШ§ЩӢ ШўШ®ЩҺШұЩҺ** (So do not invoke any other god besides Allah) until the end of the addresses specific to him ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ, which branch off from His saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҺШӘЩ’ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ** etc., as will be explained.

The speech was directed to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ rather than the people because it is reasoned with what they do not accept due to their disbeliefвҖ”namely His saying: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Щ’Ш№Щҗ Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ№Щ’ШІЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (they are indeed barred from hearing). The "devil" (Ш§Щ„ШҙЩҠШ·Ш§ЩҶ) is the evil one, and its plural is "devils" (Ш§Щ„ШҙЩҠШ§Ш·ЩҠЩҶ), and what is meant by them are the evil ones among the jinn.

His saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ЩҠЩҺЩҶЩ’ШЁЩҺШәЩҗЩҠ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’** вҖ” *It does not behoove them (211)*; meaning the devils. It is said in *al-MajmaвҖҳ*: "The meaning of the Arabs' saying 'it behooves you (ЩҠЩҶШЁШәЩҠ Щ„Щғ) to do such and such' is that you are requested to do it by the requirement of the intellect, from *al-bughyah* which is the request." [End of quote].

The reason why it does not behoove them to bring it down is that they are an evil creation whose only concern is evil, corruption, taking up falsehood, and depicting it in the form of truth to lead astray from the path of Allah. The Quran is the speech of truth (ЩғЩ„Ш§Щ… ШӯЩӮ) to which falsehood has no way; thus, it does not suit their diabolical nature to deliver it to anyone.

His saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’ШӘЩҺШ·ЩҗЩҠШ№ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...nor are they able to (211)*; meaning they are not capable of bringing it down because it is heavenly speech received by the angels from the Lord of Might, and they bring it down by His command under preservation and guardianship from Him, the Exalted, as He said: **ЩҒЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’Щ„ЩҸЩғЩҸ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШЁЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҶЩҗ ЩҠЩҺШҜЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗ ЩҲЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ Ш®ЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҒЩҗЩҮЩҗ ШұЩҺШөЩҺШҜШ§ЩӢ Щ„ЩҗЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҺ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ ШЈЩҺШЁЩ’Щ„ЩҺШәЩҸЩҲШ§ ШұЩҗШіШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘЩҗ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺШӯШ§Ш·ЩҺ ШЁЩҗЩ…Ш§ Щ„ЩҺШҜЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’** вҖ” *...He dispatches a sentinel before him and behind him, so that He may know that they have delivered the messages of their Lord, and He encompasses all that is with them (al-Jinn: 27-28).* To this, His saying points: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Щ’Ш№Щҗ** etc.

His saying: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Щ’Ш№Щҗ Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ№Щ’ШІЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *Indeed they are barred from hearing [it] (212)*; meaning that the devils are barred from hearing heavenly news and becoming aware of what occurs in the Highest Assembly (Ш§Щ„Щ…Щ„ШҘ Ш§Щ„ШЈШ№Щ„Щү), as they are pelted with piercing flames (Ш§Щ„ШҙЩҮШЁ Ш§Щ„Ш«Ш§ЩӮШЁШ©) if they try to listen, as Allah mentioned in various places of His speech.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҒЩҺЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩҺШҜЩ’Ш№ЩҸ Щ…ЩҺШ№ЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҮШ§ЩӢ ШўШ®ЩҺШұЩҺ ЩҒЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШ№ЩҺШ°Щ‘ЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *So do not invoke any other god besides Allah, lest you should be among the punished (213).* An address to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ forbidding him from polytheism (Ш§Щ„ШҙШұЩғ) with Allah, branching off from His saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҺШӘЩ’ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҺЩҠШ§Ш·ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҸ** etc. That is: If this Quran is a revelation from the Lord of all the worlds and the devils did not bring it down, and it forbids polytheism and promises punishment for it, then do not associate anything with Allah, lest the promised punishment reach you and you enter the group of the punished.

The fact that he ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ is protected by a divine infallibility (Ш№ШөЩ…Ш©) вҖ” *the divinely granted protection from sin and error* вҖ” with which the issuance of sin from him is impossible, does not contradict his being forbidden from polytheism. For infallibility does not necessitate the invalidation of the command and prohibition's connection to the infallible, nor the removal of legal obligation from him insofar as he is a human possessing choice in action and refraining, for whom obedience and disobedience are conceivable in relation to himself. Verses regarding the legal obligation of the prophets (Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…) are numerous in the Noble Quran, such as His saying regarding the prophets (Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…): **ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ ШЈЩҺШҙЩ’ШұЩҺЩғЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҺШӯЩҺШЁЩҗШ·ЩҺ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...and had they ascribed any partners [to Him], what they used to do would have come to naught (al-An'am: 88)*, and His saying to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ: **Щ„ЩҺШҰЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШЈЩҺШҙЩ’ШұЩҺЩғЩ’ШӘЩҺ Щ„ЩҺЩҠЩҺШӯЩ’ШЁЩҺШ·ЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺ Ш№ЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩғЩҺ** вҖ” *...if you ascribe a partner [to Allah], your deeds will surely come to naught (az-Zumar: 65).* Both verses carry the meaning of prohibition.

The saying of someвҖ”that legal obligation is for the sake of completion (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩғЩ…ЩҠЩ„), so it is removed upon the attainment and realization of perfection because it is impossible to seek what is already attainedвҖ”is an error. For the righteous deeds to which legal obligations are connected are among the effects of the sought perfection. Just as spiritual perfection (Ш§Щ„ЩғЩ…Ш§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҒШіШ§ЩҶЩҠ) must be acquired by performing its effects, practicing the deeds that suit it, and training through them, it must likewise be maintained through them. As long as the human is a human with a connection to earthly life, he has no escape from bearing the burdens of legal obligation. Discussion regarding this meaning has already preceded in some of the researches.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШҙЩҗЩҠШұЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩӮЩ’ШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *And warn your nearest relations (214).* In *al-MajmaвҖҳ*: "A man's *вҖҳashД«rah* is his kin; they were named so because he associates with them and they associate with him (*yaвҖҳДҒshiruhum*)." [End of quote]. He specifically mentioned his kin and nearest relations after forbidding himself from polytheism and warning him, to draw attention to the fact that there is no exception in the religious call, nor any compromise or leniency, as is customary in royal traditions. There is no difference in the connection of the warning between the Prophet and his nation, nor between relatives and strangers; all are servants and Allah is their Master.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҲЩҺШ§Ш®Щ’ЩҒЩҗШ¶Щ’ Ш¬ЩҺЩҶШ§ШӯЩҺЩғЩҺ Щ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩҗ Ш§ШӘЩ‘ЩҺШЁЩҺШ№ЩҺЩғЩҺ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...and lower your wing to the faithful who follow you (215)*; meaning occupy yourself with those who believe in you, gather them, and join them to yourself with kindness and mercy, just as a bird gathers its chicks to itself by lowering its wing to them. This is from the metaphor by metonymy (Ш§Щ„Ш§ШіШӘШ№Ш§ШұШ© ШЁШ§Щ„ЩғЩҶШ§ЩҠШ©); its like has preceded in His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШ§Ш®Щ’ЩҒЩҗШ¶Щ’ Ш¬ЩҺЩҶШ§ШӯЩҺЩғЩҺ Щ„ЩҗЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...and lower your wing to the faithful (al-Hijr: 88).*

What is meant by "following" (Ш§Щ„Ш§ШӘШЁШ§Ш№) is obedience, by the evidence of His saying in the following verse: **ЩҒЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШөЩҺЩҲЩ’ЩғЩҺ ЩҒЩҺЩӮЩҸЩ„Щ’ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҗЩҠ ШЁЩҺШұЩҗЩҠШЎЩҢ Щ…ЩҗЩ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *But if they disobey you, say, вҖҳI am indeed absolved of what you doвҖҷ (216).* The summary of the meaning of the two verses is: If they believe in you and follow you, then gather them to yourself with kindness and occupy yourself with their upbringing; and if they disobey you, then disassociate yourself from their action.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҲЩҺШӘЩҺЩҲЩҺЩғЩ‘ЩҺЩ„Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩү Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺШІЩҗЩҠШІЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШұЩ‘ЩҺШӯЩҗЩҠЩ…Щҗ** вҖ” *And put your trust in the All-mighty, the All-merciful (217)*; meaning you have nothing of the matter of their obedience or disobedience beyond what We have obligated you with. So leave everything beyond that to Allah, the Glorified; for by His might (Ш№ШІШӘЩҮ) He will punish the disobedient, and by His mercy (ШұШӯЩ…ШӘЩҮ) He will save the following believers.

In the specific choice of the names the All-mighty (Ш§Щ„Ш№ШІЩҠШІ) and the All-merciful (Ш§Щ„ШұШӯЩҠЩ…), there is a drawing of the mind to the stories that preceded, each of which was concluded one after another with these two noble names.

It is in the sense of saying: Put your trust in the matter of the followers and the disobedient alike in Allah; for He is the All-mighty, the All-merciful, who did to the people of Noah, Hud, Salih, Abraham, Lot, Shu'ayb, and the people of Pharaoh what He did of what We have narrated. His sunnah (ШіЩҶШ©) вҖ” *the established and unalterable way of Allah's action in creation* вҖ” is the seizing of the disobedient and the saving of the believers.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **Ш§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠ ЩҠЩҺШұШ§ЩғЩҺ ШӯЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШӘЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ…ЩҸ ЩҲЩҺШӘЩҺЩӮЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҸШЁЩҺЩғЩҺ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺШ§Ш¬ЩҗШҜЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *...who sees you when you stand [for prayer], and your transitions among those who prostrate (218-219).* The apparent meaning of the two versesвҖ”according to what first comes to mindвҖ”is that what is meant by "those who prostrate" (Ш§Щ„ШіШ§Ш¬ШҜЩҠЩҶ) are those who prostrate in prayer among the believers, among whom was the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ in his leading them in congregational prayer. What is meant, by the evidence of the contrast, is standing in prayer. The meaning would be: Who sees you while you are under His eye in the two states of your standing and your prostrating, transitioning among those who prostrate while you are praying with the believers.

Regarding the meaning of the verse, there are narrations from the paths of the Shi'a and the Sunnis which we will address in the coming traditional research (Ш§Щ„ШЁШӯШ« Ш§Щ„ШұЩҲШ§ШҰЩҠ), Allah willing.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸ ЩҮЩҸЩҲЩҺ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠШ№ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҸ** вҖ” *Indeed He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing (220).* A rationale for His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШӘЩҺЩҲЩҺЩғЩ‘ЩҺЩ„Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩү Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺШІЩҗЩҠШІЩҗ Ш§Щ„ШұЩ‘ЩҺШӯЩҗЩҠЩ…Щҗ**.

In the versesвҖ”according to what has preceded of their meaningвҖ”is a consolation for the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ, glad tidings for the believers of salvation, and a threat to the disbelievers of punishment.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҮЩҺЩ„Щ’ ШЈЩҸЩҶЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗШҰЩҸЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү Щ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҸ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҺЩҠШ§Ш·ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҸ** вҖ” *Should I inform you on whom the devils descend? (221)* вҖ” until His saying вҖ” **ЩғШ§Ш°ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (are liars). A definition of those upon whom the devils descend by the attribute that characterizes them, so that it may be known that the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ is not among them, nor is the Quran from the promptings of the devils. The address is directed to the polytheists.

So His saying: **ЩҮЩҺЩ„Щ’ ШЈЩҸЩҶЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗШҰЩҸЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү Щ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҸ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҺЩҠШ§Ш·ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҸ** вҖ” is in the sense of: Shall I make known to you those upon whom the devils of the jinn descend with news?

His saying: **ШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү ЩғЩҸЩ„Щ‘Щҗ ШЈЩҺЩҒЩ‘ЩҺШ§ЩғЩҚ ШЈЩҺШ«ЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҚ** вҖ” *They descend on every lying sinner (222).* It is said in *al-MajmaвҖҳ*: "The *affДҒk* is the liar; the origin of *ifk* is turning, and the *affДҒk* is one who frequently turns the news from the side of truth to the side of falsehood. The *athД«m* is the doer of the base; it is said *athima yaвҖҷthamu ithman* when he commits the base, and *taвҖҷaththama* when he leaves the sin." [End of quote].

This is because the devils have no concern except manifesting falsehood in the form of truth and beautifying the base in the dress of the good; thus, they do not descend except upon a lying sinner.

His saying: **ЩҠЩҸЩ„Щ’ЩӮЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Щ’Ш№ЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩғЩ’Ш«ЩҺШұЩҸЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩғШ§Ш°ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *They impart what they have heard, and most of them are liars (223).* It is apparent that the two plural pronouns in **ЩҠЩҸЩ„Щ’ЩӮЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (they impart) and **ШЈЩҺЩғЩ’Ш«ЩҺШұЩҸЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’** (most of them) both refer to the devils. "Hearing" (Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…Ш№) is a verbal noun meaning "that which is heard," and what is meant by it is what the devils heard of the news of heaven, even if incomplete; for they are prevented from listening and pelted with flames, so what they snatch away cannot be anything but incomplete, neither finished nor perfect, and therefore falsehood frequently seeps into it.

His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩғЩ’Ш«ЩҺШұЩҸЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩғШ§Ш°ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” meaning most of the devils are liars who do not report truth at all; this is the "most" in terms of individuals. It is also possible that what is meant is "most" in terms of the descent; meaning most of those among them who descend are liars, i.e., most of their reports are false.

The summary of the argument of the three verses is that the devils, because their nature is built upon evil, do not descend except upon every lying profligate, and most of them are liars in their reports. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ is not a lying sinner, nor is the speech revealed to him a fabricated lie. Thus, he is not among those upon whom the devils descend, nor is the one who descends upon him a devil, nor is the Quran revealed to him from the promptings of the devils.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҸШ№ЩҺШұШ§ШЎЩҸ ЩҠЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШЁЩҗШ№ЩҸЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШәШ§ЩҲЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *As for the poets, [only] the erring follow them (224)* вҖ” until His saying вҖ” **Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҺЩҒЩ’Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (they do not do). A response to the polytheists' charging the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ with being a poet. He drew attention to it after the response to their saying that he has a devil who reveals the Quran to him.

These twoвҖ”namely their saying that there is one of the jinn who comes to him, and their saying that he is a poetвҖ”were among what they used to repeat on their tongues in Mecca before the Migration to repel the true call. This is among what supports the descent of these verses in Mecca, contrary to what has been said that they were revealed in Medina.

Furthermore, the verses contain the conclusion of the Surah, namely His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШіЩҺЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШёЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸЩҲШ§ ШЈЩҺЩҠЩ‘ЩҺ Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’ЩӮЩҺЩ„ЩҺШЁЩҚ ЩҠЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩӮЩҺЩ„ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (And the wrongdoers will soon know at what goal they will end up). There is no meaning in a Surah that is among the oldest Meccan Surahs remaining for years in a state of incompleteness, then being completed in Medina. Nor is there any indication in the exception that the excepted ones are the poets of the believers after the Migration.

In any case, error (Ш§Щ„ШәЩҠ) is the opposite of right guidance (Ш§Щ„ШұШҙШҜ), which is hitting upon the reality. The rightly guided (Ш§Щ„ШұШҙЩҠШҜ) is he who only concerns himself with what is true and real, and the erring (Ш§Щ„ШәЩҲЩҠ) is he who walks the path of falsehood and misses the way of truth. Error and wandering (Ш§Щ„ШәЩҲШ§ЩҠШ©) are among what characterizes the craft of poetry, which is built upon imagination (Ш§Щ„ШӘШ®ЩҠЩҠЩ„) and depicting the non-real in the form of the real. Therefore, only the erring person, infatuated with imaginative decorations and illusory depictions that distract from the truth and divert from right guidance, concerns himself with it. None follow the poets whose craft is built upon error and wandering except the erring; and that is His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҸШ№ЩҺШұШ§ШЎЩҸ ЩҠЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШЁЩҗШ№ЩҸЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШәШ§ЩҲЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ**.

His saying: **ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…Щ’ ШӘЩҺШұЩҺ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ ЩғЩҸЩ„Щ‘Щҗ ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҚ ЩҠЩҺЩҮЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ…Ш§ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҺЩҒЩ’Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *Do you not see that they roam in every valley, and that they say what they do not do? (225-226).* It is said *hДҒma yahД«mu hayamДҒnan* when one wanders aimlessly. What is meant by their roaming in every valley is their lack of restraint in speech without stopping at any limit; they might praise the censured falsehood just as the praised truth is praised, and they might lampoon the beautiful just as the ugly and base is lampooned, and they might call toward falsehood and divert from the truth. In that is a deviation from the path of the human primordial nature built upon right guidance and calling toward the truth. Likewise, their saying what they do not do is a deviation from the path of the primordial nature.

The summary of the argument of the three verses is that he ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ is not a poet, because the poets are followed by the erring due to their craft being built upon error and the opposite of right guidance. But those who follow him only follow him seeking right guidance and hitting upon the reality, and seeking the truth, because what he possesses of speech encompassing the call is built upon truth and right guidance rather than falsehood and error.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШўЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҲЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ‘ЩҺШ§Щ„ЩҗШӯШ§ШӘЩҗ ЩҲЩҺШ°ЩҺЩғЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҺ ЩғЩҺШ«ЩҗЩҠШұШ§ЩӢ** вҖ” *...excepting those who have faith, do righteous deeds, and remember Allah much (227)* etc. An exception from the censured poets. The excepted ones are the poets of the believers; for faith and righteous deeds naturally deter a person from leaving the truth and following falsehood. Then, the frequent remembrance of Allah, the Glorified, makes a person mindful of Him, the Exalted, turning toward the truth that He approves and turning away from the falsehood that He does not love to be occupied with. Thus, what happened to those does not happen to these.

Through this explanation, the reason for qualifying the excepted ones with faith and the performance of righteous deeds, then conjoining His saying **ЩҲЩҺШ°ЩҺЩғЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҺ ЩғЩҺШ«ЩҗЩҠШұШ§ЩӢ** (and remember Allah much) to that, becomes clear.

His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШ§ЩҶЩ’ШӘЩҺШөЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШЁЩҺШ№Щ’ШҜЩҗ Щ…Ш§ ШёЩҸЩ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҸЩҲШ§** вҖ” *...and help themselves after they have been wronged (227).* "Helping themselves" (Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩҶШӘШөШ§Шұ) means taking revenge. It has been said: What is meant by it is the response of the poets among the believers to the polytheists' poems in which they lampooned the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ or attacked the religion and disparaged Islam and the Muslims. This is a good view supported by the context.

His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШіЩҺЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШёЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸЩҲШ§ ШЈЩҺЩҠЩ‘ЩҺ Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’ЩӮЩҺЩ„ЩҺШЁЩҚ ЩҠЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩӮЩҺЩ„ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *And the wrongdoers will soon know at what goal they will end up (227).* The term **Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҶЩӮЩ„ШЁ** is a noun of place or a mim-infinitive. The meaning is: (*And the wrongdoers will soon know*)вҖ”and they are the polytheists according to what the context providesвҖ”to what place of return and departure they shall return and depart, which is the Fire; or what kind of overturning they shall be overturned.

In this is a threat to the polytheists and a return of the conclusion of the Surah to its opening; for at its beginning occurred His saying: **ЩҒЩҺЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ ЩғЩҺШ°Щ‘ЩҺШЁЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҺШіЩҺЩҠЩҺШЈЩ’ШӘЩҗЩҠЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШЁШӨЩҸШ§ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’ШӘЩҺЩҮЩ’ШІЩҗШӨЩҸЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” *They have already denied [it], so soon there will come to them the news of what they have been deriding (6).*

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain of transmission from al-Hajjal, from one whom he mentioned, from one of the two [Imams] (peace be upon them), he said: I asked him about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **ШЁЩҗЩ„ЩҗШіШ§ЩҶЩҚ Ш№ЩҺШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩҚ Щ…ЩҸШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ** (in a clear Arabic language). He said: **ЩҠШЁЩҠЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩ„ШіЩҶ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШӘШЁЩҠЩҶЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩ„ШіЩҶ** вҖ” "It clarifies the [other] tongues, but the [other] tongues do not clarify it."

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, regarding His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ ЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„Щү ШЁЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¶Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** etc. Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: **Щ„ЩҲ ЩҶШІЩ„ЩҶШ§ Ш§Щ„ЩӮШұШўЩҶ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш¬Щ… Щ…Ш§ ШўЩ…ЩҶШӘ ШЁЩҮ Ш§Щ„Ш№ШұШЁ ЩҖ ЩҲЩӮШҜ ЩҶШІЩ„ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„Ш№ШұШЁ ЩҒШўЩ…ЩҶШӘ ШЁЩҮ Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш¬Щ… ЩҖ ЩҒЩҮШ°ЩҮ ЩҒШ¶ЩҠЩ„Ш© Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш¬Щ…** вҖ” "Had We sent down the Quran to the non-Arabs, the Arabs would not have believed in itвҖ”but it was sent down to the Arabs and the non-Arabs believed in itвҖ”so this is a virtue of the non-Arabs."

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain of transmission from вҖҳAli ibn вҖҳIsa al-Qammat, from his uncle, from Abu вҖҳAbdillah (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ was shown in his sleep the UmayyadsвҖ”climbing upon his pulpit after himвҖ”and leading the people astray from the path backwardвҖ”so he woke up distressed and sorrowful.

He said: Then Gabriel descended and said: "O Messenger of Allah, why do I see you distressed and sorrowful?" He said: "O Gabriel, I saw the Umayyads this night of mineвҖ”climbing my pulpit after meвҖ”leading the people astray from the path backward." He said: "By the One who sent you with the truth as a prophet, I was not aware of it." Then he ascended to heavenвҖ”and it was not long before he descended to him with verses from the Quran to comfort him with them. He said: **ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҺШЈЩҺЩҠЩ’ШӘЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ Щ…ЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШ№Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШіЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ЩҖ Ш«ЩҸЩ…Щ‘ЩҺ Ш¬Ш§ШЎЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩҸЩҲШ№ЩҺШҜЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩҖ Щ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺШәЩ’ЩҶЩү Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩҸЩ…ЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШ№ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (Tell Me, if We let them enjoy for some years, then there comes to them what they were promised, what they were given to enjoy will not avail them). And He sent down to him: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШІЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ Щ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’Щ„ЩҺШ©Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ШұЩҗ ЩҖ ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺШҜЩ’ШұШ§ЩғЩҺ Щ…Ш§ Щ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’Щ„ЩҺШ©ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ШұЩҗ ЩҖ Щ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’Щ„ЩҺШ©ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ШұЩҗ Ш®ЩҺЩҠЩ’ШұЩҢ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШЈЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҒЩҗ ШҙЩҺЩҮЩ’ШұЩҚ** (Indeed We sent it down on the Night of Ordainment. And what will show you what is the Night of Ordainment? The Night of Ordainment is better than a thousand months). Allah made the Night of Ordainment for His Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮвҖ”better than a thousand months of the Umayyads' rule.

In *ad-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Abi Hatim extracted from Abu Jahdam, he said: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ was seen as if he were bewildered, so they asked him about that. He said: **ЩҲЩ„Щ… ЩҲШұШЈЩҠШӘ Ш№ШҜЩҲЩҠ ЩҠЩ„ЩҲЩҶ ШЈЩ…Шұ ШЈЩ…ШӘЩҠ Щ…ЩҶ ШЁШ№ШҜЩҠ** вҖ” "And why [not], when I have seen my enemy taking charge of the affair of my nation after me?" Then there was revealed: **ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҺШЈЩҺЩҠЩ’ШӘЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ Щ…ЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШ№Щ’ЩҶШ§ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШіЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ЩҖ Ш«ЩҸЩ…Щ‘ЩҺ Ш¬Ш§ШЎЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩҸЩҲШ№ЩҺШҜЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩҖ Щ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺШәЩ’ЩҶЩү Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩҸЩ…ЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШ№ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (Tell Me, if We let them enjoy for some years, then there comes to them what they were promised, what they were given to enjoy will not avail them), and his soul was comforted.

**I say:** His saying: **ЩҲЩ„Щ… ЩҲШұШЈЩҠШӘ** etc., contains a deletion, and the estimation is: "And why should I not be so, when I have seen..." etc.

In the same source (*ad-Durr al-Manthur*), Ahmad, вҖҳAbd ibn Humayd, al-Bukhari, Muslim, al-Tirmidhi, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim, Ibn Mardawayh, and al-Bayhaqi in *ShuвҖҳab al-Iman* and *ad-DalaвҖҷil* narrated from Abu Hurayrah, who said: "When this verse was revealed: **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШҙЩҗЩҠШұЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩӮЩ’ШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (And warn your nearest relations), the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ summoned the Quraysh, addressing them both generally and specifically. He said: 'O assembly of Quraysh! Save yourselves from the Fire, for I possess for you neither harm nor benefit. O assembly of the children of KaвҖҳb ibn LuвҖҷay! Save yourselves from the Fire, for I possess for you neither harm nor benefit. O assembly of the children of Qusay! Save yourselves from the Fire, for I possess for you neither harm nor benefit. O assembly of the children of вҖҳAbd Manaf! Save yourselves from the Fire, for I possess for you neither harm nor benefit. O children of вҖҳAbd al-Muttalib! Save yourselves from the Fire, for I possess for you neither harm nor benefit. O Fatimah, daughter of Muhammad! Save yourself from the Fire, for I possess for you neither harm nor benefit. Behold, you have ties of kinship (ШұШӯЩ…Ш§ЩӢ) which I shall uphold with their due rights (ШіШЈШЁЩ„ЩҮШ§ ШЁШЁЩ„Ш§Щ„ЩҮШ§).'"

In the same source, вҖҳAbd ibn Humayd and Ibn Mardawayh narrated from Ibn вҖҳAbbas, who said: "When **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШҙЩҗЩҠШұЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩӮЩ’ШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (And warn your nearest relations) was revealed, he began calling them tribe by tribe."

In the same source, SaвҖҳid ibn Mansur, al-Bukhari, Ibn Mardawayh, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated from Ibn вҖҳAbbas, who said: "When **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШҙЩҗЩҠШұЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩӮЩ’ШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (And warn your nearest relations) [and your sincere kinsmen among them] was revealed, the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ went out until he climbed Mount al-Safa and called out: 'O people, a morning raid!' (ЩҠШ§ ШөШЁШ§ШӯШ§ЩҮ). They asked: 'Who is this calling out?' They said: 'It is Muhammad.' So they gathered around him. If a man was unable to go out himself, he would send a messenger to see what it was.

Abu Lahab and the Quraysh arrived, and he ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ said: 'Tell me, if I were to inform you that there are horsemen in the valley intending to raid you, would you believe me?' They replied: 'Yes, we have never experienced anything from you but truthfulness.' He said: 'Then I am a warner to you before a severe punishment.' Abu Lahab said: 'Perdition to you for the rest of the day! Is it for this that you have gathered us?' Then there was revealed: **ШӘЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҺШӘЩ’ ЩҠЩҺШҜШ§ ШЈЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҺШЁЩҚ ЩҲЩҺШӘЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҺ** (Perish the hands of Abu Lahab, and perish he!)."

In the same source, al-Tabarani and Ibn Mardawayh narrated from Abu Umamah, who said: "When **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШҙЩҗЩҠШұЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩӮЩ’ШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (And warn your nearest relations) was revealed, the Messenger of Allah gathered the Banu Hashim and seated them at the door, and he gathered his wives and family and seated them in the house. Then he looked upon them and said: 'O Banu Hashim! Buy your souls from the Fire and strive for the liberation of your necks; ransom them yourselves from Allah, for I possess nothing for you against Allah.'

Then he turned to his household (ШЈЩҮЩ„ ШЁЩҠШӘЩҮ) and said: 'O вҖҳAвҖҷishah daughter of Abu Bakr, O Hafsah daughter of вҖҳUmar, O Umm Salamah, O Fatimah daughter of Muhammad, and O Umm al-Zubayr, aunt of the Messenger of Allah! Buy your souls from Allah and strive for the liberation of your necks, for I possess nothing for you against Allah, nor can I avail you anything...'" (the tradition continues).

**I say:** Other narrations carry the same meaning as these reports. In some of them, it is mentioned that he ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ specifically warned the Banu вҖҳAbd Manaf, which would include both the Banu Umayyah and the Banu Hashim.

However, the first three narrations do not align with the verse, as they generalize the warning to the Quraysh at large, whereas the verse explicitly mentions the "nearest relations" (Ш§Щ„Ш№ШҙЩҠШұШ© Ш§Щ„ШЈЩӮШұШЁЩҠЩҶ), who are either the Banu вҖҳAbd al-Muttalib or the Banu Hashim.
