The furthest narration from the verse is the second one, which states that he began calling them tribe by tribe.

Furthermore, the previously established meaning of the verseвҖ”which is the negation that kinship to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ can substitute for godwariness (ШӘЩӮЩҲЩү) toward AllahвҖ”is alluded to in these narrations where he says: "I cannot avail you anything against Allah." This meaning is not suitable in its generality for those other than his close relatives ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ.

As for the fourth narration, the verse **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШҙЩҗЩҠШұЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩӮЩ’ШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (And warn your nearest relations) is a Meccan verse in a Meccan Surah, and no one has claimed that this verse was revealed in Medina. Where were вҖҳAвҖҷishah, Hafsah, and Umm Salamah on the day of its revelation? The Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ did not marry them until he was in Medina. Therefore, the reliable narrations are those indicating that he ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ specifically warned the Banu Hashim or the Banu вҖҳAbd al-Muttalib on the day the verse was revealed. It is strange that al-Alusi, after citing these narrations, remarked: "If all are authentic, the way to reconcile them is to say that the warning occurred multiple times."

In *al-MajmaвҖҳ*, from the *Tafsir* of al-ThaвҖҳlabi with his chain of transmission from BaraвҖҷ ibn вҖҳAzib, who said: "When this verse was revealed, the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ gathered the Banu вҖҳAbd al-Muttalib, who were forty men at that timeвҖ”a man among them could eat a young sheep and drink a large vessel of milk (Ш§Щ„Ш№Ші). He ordered вҖҳAli to prepare a leg of mutton, which he seasoned. Then he said: 'Approach in the Name of Allah.' The people approached ten by ten and ate until they were satisfied. Then he called for a bowl of milk, took a sip from it, and said to them: 'Drink in the Name of Allah.' They drank until their thirst was quenched. Abu Lahab forestalled him and said: 'This is what the man has bewitched you with!' So the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ remained silent that day and did not speak.

The next day, he invited them again for similar food and drink. Then the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ warned them, saying: 'O Banu вҖҳAbd al-Muttalib! I am indeed the warner to you from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic; so submit (ШЈШіЩ„Щ…ЩҲШ§) and obey me, and you shall be guided.'

Then he said: 'Who will brother me, assist me (ЩҠЩҲШ§ШІШұЩҶЩҠ), and be my supporter (ЩҲЩ„ЩҠЩҠ), my legatee (ЩҲШөЩҠЩҠ) after me, and my successor (Ш®Щ„ЩҠЩҒШӘЩҠ) among my family, and pay my debt?' The people remained silent. He repeated it three times, and each time the people remained silent while вҖҳAli said: 'I will.' On the third time, he said: 'You are the one.' The people stood up, saying to Abu Talib: 'Obey your son, for he has been made a commander over you!'"

Al-Tabarsi said: "This story is also narrated from Abu RafiвҖҳ, stating that he gathered them in the mountain pass (Ш§Щ„ШҙШ№ШЁ). He prepared a leg of mutton for them, and they ate until they were full, and he gave them a large vessel of milk to drink, and they all drank until their thirst was quenched. Then he said: 'Indeed, Allah has commanded me to warn my kinsmen and my group. Allah has never sent a prophet except that He appointed for him from his family a brother, a vizier (ЩҲШІЩҠШұШ§ЩӢ), an heir (ЩҲШ§ШұШ«Ш§ЩӢ), a legatee (ЩҲШөЩҠШ§ЩӢ), and a successor (Ш®Щ„ЩҠЩҒШ©) among his family. Which of you, then, will stand and pledge allegiance to me on the basis that he is my brother, my heir, my vizier, and my legatee, and that he shall be to me as Aaron was to Moses?' вҖҳAli said: 'I will.' He said: 'Come close to me.' Then he opened his mouth and applied some of his saliva into вҖҳAli's mouth and spat between his shoulder blades and his chest. Abu Lahab said: 'What a wretched gift you have given your cousinвҖ”that he answered you and you filled his mouth and face with spittle!' The Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ replied: 'I have filled him with wisdom and knowledge.'"

**I say:** Al-Suyuti narrated in *ad-Durr al-Manthur* the substance of the tradition of al-BaraвҖҷ from Ibn Ishaq, Ibn Jarir, Ibn Abi Hatim, Ibn Mardawayh, Abu NuвҖҳaym, and al-Bayhaqi in *ad-DalaвҖҷil* through various paths from вҖҳAli (may Allah be pleased with him). In it, the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ spoke and said: "O Banu вҖҳAbd al-Muttalib! By Allah, I do not know of anyone among the Arabs who has brought his people something better than what I have brought you. I have brought you the good of this world and the Hereafter, and Allah has commanded me to call you to Him. Which of you, then, will assist me in this matter of mine?" вҖҳAli said: "I said, though I was the youngest of them: 'I will.' The people then stood up laughing."

In *вҖҳIlal al-SharaвҖҷiвҖҳ*, with its chain of transmission from вҖҳAbdullah ibn al-Harith ibn Nawfal from вҖҳAli ibn Abi Talib Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…, who said: "When **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШҙЩҗЩҠШұЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩӮЩ’ШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (And warn your nearest relations)вҖ”meaning your sincere kinsmenвҖ”was revealed, the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ summoned the Banu вҖҳAbd al-Muttalib, who were forty men at that time, give or take one. He said: 'Which of you will be my brother, my heir, my vizier, my legatee, and my successor among you after me?' He presented this to them man by man, and all of them refused until he came to вҖҳAli. I said: 'I will, O Messenger of Allah.'

He said: 'O Banu вҖҳAbd al-Muttalib! This is my heir, my vizier, and my successor among you after me.' The people stood up laughing at one another and saying to Abu Talib: 'He has commanded you to hear and obey this boy!'"

**I say:** It is possible to infer from his Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… saying, "meaning your sincere kinsmen," that what was attributed to the recitation (ЩӮШұШ§ШЎШ©) of the Ahl al-BaytвҖ”**ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҗШұЩ’ Ш№ЩҺШҙЩҗЩҠШұЩҺШӘЩҺЩғЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩӮЩ’ШұЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (And warn your nearest relations) [your sincere kinsmen among them]вҖ”and also attributed to the codex of Ubayy ibn KaвҖҳb, was in the nature of an interpretation (ШӘЩҒШіЩҠШұ).

In *al-MajmaвҖҳ*, regarding His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺШӘЩҺЩӮЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҸШЁЩҺЩғЩҺ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺШ§Ш¬ЩҗШҜЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** (and your moving about among those who prostrate), it is said: It means your transition among the prostrating monotheists (Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҲШӯШҜЩҠЩҶ) from prophet to prophet until He brought you forth as a prophet. This is narrated from Ibn вҖҳAbbas in the report of вҖҳAtaвҖҷ and вҖҳIkrimah, and it is what is narrated from Abu JaвҖҳfar and Abu вҖҳAbdillah Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…, who both said: "The loins of the prophets, prophet after prophet, until He brought him forth from the loin of his father through marriage, not through fornication, since the time of Adam."

**I say:** This has been narrated by other ShiвҖҳi narrators, and it was narrated in *ad-Durr al-Manthur* from Ibn Abi Hatim, Ibn Mardawayh, Abu NuвҖҳaym, and others from Ibn вҖҳAbbas and others.

In *al-MajmaвҖҳ*, Jabir narrated from Abu JaвҖҳfar Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ said: 'Do not rise before me and do not descend before me, for I see you from behind me just as I see you from in front of me.' Then he recited this verse."

**I say:** He ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ refers to placing the forehead on the ground and raising it during prostration (Ш§Щ„ШіШ¬ШҜШ©). This was also narrated in *ad-Durr al-Manthur* from Ibn вҖҳAbbas and others.

In *ad-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Abi Shaybah and Ahmad narrated from Abu SaвҖҳid, who said: "While we were traveling with the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ, a poet appeared reciting poetry. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ said: 'For the belly of one of you to be filled with pus is better for him than to be filled with poetry.'"

**I say:** This is also narrated through ShiвҖҳi paths from Imam al-Sadiq Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… from him (the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ).

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, it is said: "They admonish people but do not take admonition themselves; they forbid evil but do not desist from it; they enjoin good but do not act upon it. They are those about whom Allah said: **ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩ…Щ’ ШӘЩҺШұЩҺ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ ЩғЩҸЩ„Щ‘Щҗ ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҚ ЩҠЩҺЩҮЩҗЩҠЩ…ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (Do you not see that they roam in every valley?)вҖ”meaning they follow every pathвҖ”**ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ…Ш§ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҺЩҒЩ’Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (and that they say what they do not do?). They are those who usurped the right of the Progeny of Muhammad."

In the *IвҖҳtiqadat* of al-Saduq, Imam al-Sadiq Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… was asked about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҸШ№ЩҺШұШ§ШЎЩҸ ЩҠЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺШЁЩҗШ№ЩҸЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШәШ§ЩҲЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** (As for the poets, [only] the perverse follow them). He said: "They are the storytellers (Ш§Щ„ЩӮШөШ§Шө)."

**I say:** They are among the instances (Ш§Щ„Щ…ШөШ§ШҜЩҠЩӮ) of the verse, and the comprehensive meaning is what was mentioned previously in the commentary on the verse.

In *ad-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Abi Shaybah narrated from Ibn MasвҖҳud from the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ, who said: "Indeed, in some poetry there is wisdom (ШӯЩғЩ…Ш§ЩӢ), and in some eloquence (Ш§Щ„ШЁЩҠШ§ЩҶ) there is sorcery (ШіШӯШұШ§ЩӢ)."

**I say:** The first part was also narrated from him, from Buraydah and Ibn вҖҳAbbas from the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ, and also from Ibn Mardawayh from Abu Hurayrah from him ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ, with the wording: "Indeed, in some poetry there is wisdom (ШӯЩғЩ…Ш©)." The praised form of poetry is that which contains support for the truth, and it is not included in the condemnation of the verse.

In *al-MajmaвҖҳ*, from al-Zuhri, who said: "вҖҳAbd al-Rahman ibn KaвҖҳb ibn Malik narrated to me that KaвҖҳb ibn Malik said: 'O Messenger of Allah, what do you say regarding the poets?' He replied: 'The believer strives with his sword and his tongue. By Him in Whose hand is my soul, it is as if you are pelting them with arrows.'"

Al-Tabarsi said: "The Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ said to Hassan ibn Thabit: 'Satirize themвҖ”or contend with them in satireвҖ”and the Holy Spirit (ШұЩҲШӯ Ш§Щ„ЩӮШҜШі) is with you.' This was narrated by al-Bukhari and Muslim in the two *Sahihs*."

In *ad-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Abi Shaybah, вҖҳAbd ibn Humayd, Abu Dawud in his *Nasikh*, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Ibn Mardawayh narrated from Abu al-Hasan Salim al-Barrad, who said: "When the verse **ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„ШҙЩ‘ЩҸШ№ЩҺШұШ§ШЎЩҸ** (As for the poets...) was revealed, вҖҳAbdullah ibn Rawahah, KaвҖҳb ibn Malik, and Hassan ibn Thabit came weeping. They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, Allah has revealed this verse, and He knows that we are poets; are we destroyed?' Then Allah revealed: **ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШўЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҲЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ‘ЩҺШ§Щ„ЩҗШӯШ§ШӘЩҗ** (except those who have faith, do righteous deeds...). So the Messenger of Allah summoned them and recited it to them."

**I say:** This narration and those similar to it are what led some to claim that the last five verses of the Surah are Medinan. You have already seen the discussion regarding this in the commentary on the verses.

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain of transmission from Abu вҖҳUbaydah from Abu вҖҳAbdillah Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…, who said: "Among the most difficult things Allah has obligated upon His creation is the frequent remembrance of Allah (Ш°ЩғШұ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩғШ«ЩҠШұШ§ЩӢ)." Then he said: "I do not mean [only] saying 'Glory be to Allah' (ШіШЁШӯШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ), 'Praise be to Allah' (Ш§Щ„ШӯЩ…ШҜ Щ„Щ„ЩҮ), 'There is no god but Allah' (Щ„Ш§ ШҘЩ„ЩҮ ШҘЩ„Ш§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ), and 'Allah is Great' (Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈЩғШЁШұ)вҖ”though that is part of itвҖ”but rather the remembrance of Allah regarding what He has made lawful and prohibited; if it is an act of obedience, he performs it, and if it is an act of disobedience, he abandons it."

**I say:** In this there is support for what was mentioned previously in the commentary on the verse.
